mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Extension of tester/Runner with custom builtins and unknowns
mission.description: Extension of tester/Runner with custom builtins and unknowns. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
I need to execute tests written in Rego using the API of the library, in particular I have modules and test as strings and so I use the library in this way: 

```
mainModule, _ := ast.ParseModuleWithOpts(policiesFilePath, PolicyFileContent,

steps.title: Apply the fix for Extension of tester/Runner with custom builtins and unknowns
steps.description:
See the detail description on: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/issues/4245
```yaml
mainModule, _ := ast.ParseModuleWithOpts(policiesFilePath, PolicyFileContent, parserOptions)
modules[policiesFilePath] = mainModule
testsModule, _ := ast.ParseModuleWithOpts(testsFilePath, TestFileContent, parserOptions)
modules[testsFilePath] = testsModule

runner := tester.NewRunner().
                SetModules(modules).
                SetTimeout(timeout).
                EnableTracing(true).
                CapturePrintOutput(true)

resultCh, _ := runner.RunTests(ctx, nil)
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Extension of tester/Runner with custom builtins and unknowns" is functioning correctly.

type BuiltinMap struct {
	Decl *ast.Builtin
	Func func(*rego.Rego)
}

type Runner struct {
	compiler              *ast.Compiler
	store                 storage.Store
	cover                 topdown.QueryTracer
	trace                 bool
	enablePrintStatements bool
	runtime               *ast.Term
	failureLine           bool
	timeout               time.Duration
	modules               map[string]*ast.Module
	bundles               map[string]*bundle.Bundle
	filter                string
	target                string // target type (wasm, rego, etc.)

	unknowns              []string  //  <--- ADDED
}
